channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Ted Cruz Comments on Spencer Pratt's Rising Support in Los Angeles Mayoral Race and Criticizes Mayor Karen Bass's Policies
item.description: Texas Senator Ted Cruz weighed in on the Los Angeles mayoral race, highlighting the growing momentum for former reality TV star Spencer Pratt as voters express dissatisfaction with current Mayor Karen...

item.title: Local Foods That Support Your Immune System During Winter
item.description: As winter chills sweep across South Africa, the risk of catching seasonal illnesses like influenza and RSV rises, especially when we spend more time indoors. Nutrition expert Omy Naidoo from Newtricio...

item.title: Dr Rassie Erasmus Awarded the Order of Ikhamanga in Gold for His Contribution to South African Rugby
item.description: Dr Rassie Erasmus, renowned for his exceptional contributions to South African rugby, has been awarded the prestigious Order of Ikhamanga in Gold by President Cyril Ramaphosa. The award, presented dur...

item.title: Senate Questions Acting AG Todd Blanche About Maxwell Prison Visit
item.description: During a Senate hearing on May 19, 2026, Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche faced scrutiny over his visit with Ghislaine Maxwell, the convicted associate of Jeffrey Epstein, which coincided with her...

item.title: Defence Highlights Coercion in Dean Penney First-Degree Murder Trial
item.description: The first-degree murder trial of Dean Penney, accused of killing his estranged wife Jennifer Hillier-Penney in late 2016, is currently in its closing argument phase at the Supreme Court of Newfoundlan...

item.title: AIIB signs $320 million loan for reconstruction of Pakistan’s N-5 highway
item.description: Islamabad: Pakistan and the Asian Infrastructure Investment Bank (AIIB) have inked a $320 million loan agreement for reconstruction and improvement of critical sections of the N-5 highway, which stret...

item.title: Andy Burnham officially selected to contest Makerfield by-election
item.description: The Labour Party has formally confirmed Andy Burnham, the current Mayor of Greater Manchester, as its candidate for the upcoming Makerfield by-election. This election was triggered by the resignation ...

item.title: Josh Johnson delivers a standout HBO special with Symphony
item.description: Josh Johnson, the American comedian and rotating host-correspondent on Comedy Central's The Daily Show, has released his debut HBO special, Symphony, following his 2023 Peacock hour, Up Here Killing M...

item.title: 2025 GMMP Report Highlights Low Representation of Women in Nigerian Media
item.description: The 2025 Global Media Monitoring Project (GMMP) report has once again highlighted the persistent underrepresentation of women in Nigerian media. Coordinated by the World Association for Christian Comm...

item.title: Historic Westminster Hotel in Dawson City destroyed by fire
item.description: The Westminster Hotel, commonly known as The Pit by Dawson City residents, was lost to a devastating fire. The building, which dates back to 1898, had been a cherished landmark and social hub in the c...

item.title: Kakamega Mother Mourns Son Who Died During Fuel Price Protests
item.description: A tragic incident has shaken Kakamega after a young man, Derrick, a student at Sigalagala National Polytechnic, died during the recent fuel price protests that hit major towns across Kenya. According ...

item.title: Ben Shapiro Discusses the Principles and Achievements of Zionism at Toronto Symposium
item.description: At a Toronto symposium focused on combating anti-Zionism, American commentator Ben Shapiro clarified what he considers the essence of Zionism. He argued that Zionism is often misunderstood as merely t...

item.title: Authorities Rescue 3-Year-Old from Abuse by Mother’s Partner in Taguig
item.description: Authorities in Taguig City successfully rescued a three-year-old child who was physically abused by the mother’s current partner on May 18, 2026. The suspect, a 28-year-old woman identified as 'Carla'...

item.title: Ned Nwoko Claims Lead in APC Delta North Senatorial Primary Amid Disputes
item.description: Senator Ned Nwoko don claim say e win the APC senatorial primary for Delta North, talk say all di results from different areas show say e crush former Governor Ifeanyi Okowa. Di announcement don trigg...

item.title: Michigan's Role in Protecting Voting Rights and Fair Representation
item.description: In April 2026, the U.S. Supreme Court made it more difficult for communities of color to challenge electoral maps that may discriminate against them, undermining protections for fair political represe...

item.title: Mphela Recommends Four-Striker Strategy for Bafana at World Cup
item.description: Former Bafana Bafana striker Katlego “Killer” Mphela has called on coach Hugo Broos to include four strikers in South Africa’s squad for the upcoming 2026 World Cup, co-hosted by the US, Mexico, and C...

item.title: Morogoro communities benefit from agroforestry and carbon trading initiatives
item.description: In Morogoro Region, Tanzania, efforts to scale up agroforestry are creating opportunities for local communities to earn from carbon trading while improving environmental conservation and household inc...

item.title: Parents Step In to Educate Children as Dulcie September Primary Remains Closed
item.description: At Dulcie September Primary School in Rabie Ridge, teaching has come to a halt for over a month due to deteriorating infrastructure. Parents have pulled their children from the school, citing unsafe c...

item.title: Kenya suspends public transport strike for a week amid fuel price talks
item.description: Kenya government announced that the public transport strike, triggered by rising fuel prices due to the Iran war, will be suspended for one week. The strike began on Monday, causing major disruption t...

item.title: USDA’s New SNAP Store Requirements May Limit Access for Low-Income Shoppers
item.description: Starting November 4, 2026, retailers accepting SNAP benefits must meet stricter requirements, including offering at least seven items in four staple food categories—dairy, produce, grains, and protein...
